nodes.label: Squirrel
nodes.name: README
nodes.title: Squirrel
nodes.summary: Local-first focus & productivity companion. Monorepo.

nodes.label: squirrel:brief
nodes.name: SKILL
nodes.title: squirrel:brief
nodes.summary: Generate a clear, structured status of a project that the user can: - Read themselves to remember where they are - Send to a stakeholder (lead, PM, client) - Use as a session-start summary - Include in a sync-out package

nodes.label: squirrel:capture
nodes.name: SKILL
nodes.title: squirrel:capture
nodes.summary: Persist transient context from the agent session into the local Markdown vault so it survives session boundaries, model context windows, and machine restarts. This is the primary "external memory" entry point for the user.

nodes.label: squirrel:challenge
nodes.name: SKILL
nodes.title: squirrel:challenge
nodes.summary: Before a decision, the most useful voice is often the user's own past self — the version who already tried this and hit a wall. This skill makes the vault **argue back**: it retrieves same-subject notes that carry a pushback signal (a decision the user reversed, a session that stalled, a capture tha

nodes.label: squirrel:connect
nodes.name: SKILL
nodes.title: squirrel:connect
nodes.summary: Cross-project links are invisible when every project is its own island. Given two conceptual TAGs, this skill surfaces the *path* between them over the vault link graph (`[[wikilinks]]`, treated as undirected) plus the notes they share — connections the user would never spot by scrolling. Read-only

nodes.label: squirrel:chunk-task
nodes.name: SKILL
nodes.title: squirrel:chunk-task
nodes.summary: A single large intent labeled "implement auth" or "refactor database layer" is invisible to the The brain — too abstract to start. This skill converts it into a concrete sequence of chunks, each ≤60 min, with domain-specific names the user recognizes.

nodes.label: squirrel:decision
nodes.name: SKILL
nodes.title: squirrel:decision
nodes.summary: Decisions get lost in chat logs. Capture them explicitly with their context, alternatives considered, and consequences — so future-self and stakeholders can understand WHY a path was taken.

nodes.label: squirrel:drift
nodes.name: SKILL
nodes.title: squirrel:drift
nodes.summary: The recurring blocker or theme showing up across *unrelated* projects is exactly what the user can't name themselves. This skill clusters recent notes by shared tags and shared `[[wikilink]]` targets, surfaces concepts that recur across 3+ notes whose **projects differ** (R-5.2), and writes a single

nodes.label: squirrel:daily
nodes.name: SKILL
nodes.title: squirrel:daily
nodes.summary: Produce a **per-directory** semantic summary of everything the user did across their AI coding agents during a time window (default: the last 24 h). One directory with 1 session or 20 sessions yields exactly **one** summary — never one per session. The digest is appended to a **weekly** note in the

nodes.label: squirrel:emerge
nodes.name: SKILL
nodes.title: squirrel:emerge
nodes.summary: Ideas rot in captures with no promotion path. `sq-think drift` names what *recurs* across unrelated projects; `sq-project emerge` asks the next question — **which recurring cluster is ripe to become a project?** It reuses drift's exact clustering (it does NOT fork it), scores each cluster, and prese

nodes.label: squirrel:ghost
nodes.name: SKILL
nodes.title: squirrel:ghost
nodes.summary: Externalizing "what would I say?" helps the user *start* (it pairs with task-initiation). But a confident wrong answer in their own voice is worse than no answer — so this skill is built around guardrails, not fluency:

nodes.label: squirrel:focus
nodes.name: SKILL
nodes.title: squirrel:focus
nodes.summary: User-driven pin of a `(project + intent)` pair into the **today** or **this-week** manual-focus slot.

nodes.label: squirrel:graduate
nodes.name: SKILL
nodes.title: squirrel:graduate
nodes.summary: Graduation is the deliberate act of turning a recurring idea into a tracked project — and it must leave a trail back to where it came from. This skill wraps the existing new-project mechanics (`POST /api/projects`, or `POST /api/post-it/<id>/convert` for a Post-it), then writes `[[wikilinks]]` from

nodes.label: squirrel:doctor
nodes.name: SKILL
nodes.title: squirrel:doctor
nodes.summary: After an install or upgrade, almost every failure a user hits maps to one of a small set of known causes: a stale background backend squatting on the port, a Gatekeeper/notarization block, a stale app bundle that won the launch race, a `PATH` gap, or a config left untouched on upgrade. This skill tu

nodes.label: squirrel:hyperfocus-guardian
nodes.name: SKILL
nodes.title: squirrel:hyperfocus-guardian
nodes.summary: Hyperfocus is a double-edged sword: productive streaks can become unsustainable tunnel vision that leads to burnout, missed meals, forgotten commitments, and poor decision-making late in a session. This skill intervenes gently but firmly to impose a circuit breaker.

nodes.label: squirrel:ingest
nodes.name: SKILL
nodes.title: squirrel:ingest
nodes.summary: External knowledge (PDFs, screenshots, stray `.md` files) never enters the vault except by manual retyping. This skill turns one file into one normal capture — tagged, provenanced, recency-marked — so it is as findable and trustworthy as a hand-written note. Extraction happens **inside the agent** v

nodes.label: squirrel:new-project (v0.1.0 — script-driven)
nodes.name: SKILL
nodes.title: squirrel:new-project (v0.1.0 — script-driven)
nodes.summary: All validation and writes live in `lib/new_project_writer.py`. This skill parses the user's request, builds the argument list, runs the script, and prints its stdout verbatim.

nodes.label: Squirrel Post-it Semantics
nodes.name: SKILL
nodes.title: Squirrel Post-it Semantics
nodes.summary: Treat Post-its as a separate capture artifact from both Quick Tasks and project/task notes.

nodes.label: squirrel-migrate-vault
nodes.name: SKILL
nodes.title: squirrel-migrate-vault
nodes.summary: Bring an existing Obsidian vault into squirrel without losing anything and without touching the original. The migrator maps free-form notes onto the structures squirrel's readers understand (projects, intents, captures, daily notes) so they show up in `/sq-status`, deadlines, and dashboards.

nodes.label: squirrel:parakeet
nodes.name: SKILL
nodes.title: squirrel:parakeet
nodes.summary: Some brains don't feel time passing — deadlines arrive as surprises. Parakeet is the friendly but persistent voice that keeps deadlines visible without shame, escalating tone only when urgency truly warrants it.

nodes.label: squirrel:reconcile
nodes.name: SKILL
nodes.title: squirrel:reconcile
nodes.summary: A vault quietly accumulates decisions and captures that contradict each other — you decided Postgres in June and Redis in July and never wrote down which won. This skill retrieves PAIRS of notes about the *same subject* (the ONE canonical definition, R-3.0), shows each conflict with a verbatim quote

nodes.label: squirrel:research
nodes.name: SKILL
nodes.title: squirrel:research
nodes.summary: Research findings evaporate the moment a chat ends. This skill turns a topic into ONE durable, findable **dossier capture** in the vault — a short summary, verbatim source citations, recency markers, and a `confidence` field — so the thinking survives the session.

nodes.label: squirrel:recover
nodes.name: SKILL
nodes.title: squirrel:recover
nodes.summary: Recover cognitive context from a forgotten session. The user closed Claude without running `/sq-end` — this skill reconstructs what happened using the edit manifest or Claude's own session history, then offers to persist it.

nodes.label: squirrel:task-initiation
nodes.name: SKILL
nodes.title: squirrel:task-initiation
nodes.summary: Task initiation is one of the most debilitating executive function deficits. The **Wall of Awful** (Mahan 2017) — the accumulated emotional weight around a task — can make starting feel physically impossible even when the user knows exactly what to do.

nodes.label: squirrel:sync-out
nodes.name: SKILL
nodes.title: squirrel:sync-out
nodes.summary: Bridge two air-gapped environments (personal/corporate) by generating a self-contained, hash-verified Markdown block. The user manually transfers via clipboard or email. The other side applies via `sync-in`.

nodes.label: squirrel:sync-in
nodes.name: SKILL
nodes.title: squirrel:sync-in
nodes.summary: Receive a context package from the OTHER environment (personal ↔ work) and apply it to the LOCAL vault safely. The user pasted the package — your job is to validate, show diff, ask, apply.

nodes.label: squirrel:where-am-i (v0.3.0 — fully script-driven)
nodes.name: SKILL
nodes.title: squirrel:where-am-i (v0.3.0 — fully script-driven)
nodes.summary: All logic, data collection, and formatting live in `lib/where_am_i_formatter.py`. The skill is a single shell call.

nodes.label: squirrel:session-start
nodes.name: SKILL
nodes.title: squirrel:session-start
nodes.summary: Solve the context-switching problem. When the user returns to a project (after hours, days, or weeks), this skill rebuilds the cognitive context FAST by reading the vault's structured notes.

nodes.label: squirrel:session-end
nodes.name: SKILL
nodes.title: squirrel:session-end
nodes.summary: Prevent context loss between sessions. Every time the user closes a coding session, capture the cognitive state in a structured shutdown note. This is the SINGLE MOST IMPORTANT skill — without it, the system fails.

nodes.label: squirrel:trace
nodes.name: SKILL
nodes.title: squirrel:trace
nodes.summary: The user cannot hold the *arc* of an idea in working memory. This skill externalizes it: given a TAG (or a topic), it gathers the relevant notes from the link graph, orders them by when they first appeared, and asks `claude -p` to render a chronological **timeline** — first appearance → key evolutio

nodes.label: Squirrel — Codex Agent Manifest
nodes.name: AGENTS
nodes.title: Squirrel — Codex Agent Manifest
nodes.summary: squirrel is a session-management plugin for engineers. It persists cognitive state across sessions, projects, and air-gapped environments via a Markdown vault. Scripts do all deterministic work; the agent only does judgment.

nodes.label: Example: Complete personal → work package
nodes.name: example-package
nodes.title: Example: Complete personal → work package
nodes.summary: - On your personal PC you researched 3 auth libraries for `TRABAJO-PROYECTO-A` - You generated a comparison table and made a preliminary decision - Now you want to take the research + decision to work

nodes.label: Squirrel — Install Guide
nodes.name: INSTALL-README
nodes.title: Squirrel — Install Guide
nodes.summary: > One-page overview of the installer: what it installs, where each component lands, and how the interactive TUI works.

nodes.label: squirrel:capture
nodes.name: SKILL
nodes.title: squirrel:capture
nodes.summary: Persist transient context from the agent session into the local Markdown vault so it survives session boundaries, model context windows, and machine restarts. This is the primary "external memory" entry point for the user.

nodes.label: squirrel:brief
nodes.name: SKILL
nodes.title: squirrel:brief
nodes.summary: Generate a clear, structured status of a project that the user can: - Read themselves to remember where they are - Send to a stakeholder (lead, PM, client) - Use as a session-start summary - Include in a sync-out package

nodes.label: squirrel:decision
nodes.name: SKILL
nodes.title: squirrel:decision
nodes.summary: Decisions get lost in chat logs. Capture them explicitly with their context, alternatives considered, and consequences — so future-self and stakeholders can understand WHY a path was taken.

nodes.label: squirrel:chunk-intent
nodes.name: SKILL
nodes.title: squirrel:chunk-intent
nodes.summary: A single large intent labeled "implement auth" or "refactor database layer" is invisible to the The brain — too abstract to start. This skill converts it into a concrete sequence of chunks, each ≤60 min, with domain-specific names the user recognizes.

nodes.label: squirrel:focus
nodes.name: SKILL
nodes.title: squirrel:focus
nodes.summary: User-driven pin of a `(project + intent)` pair into the **today** or **this-week** manual-focus slot.

nodes.label: squirrel:new-project (v0.1.0 — script-driven)
nodes.name: SKILL
nodes.title: squirrel:new-project (v0.1.0 — script-driven)
nodes.summary: All validation and writes live in `lib/new_project_writer.py`. This skill parses the user's request, builds the argument list, runs the script, and prints its stdout verbatim.

nodes.label: squirrel:parakeet
nodes.name: SKILL
nodes.title: squirrel:parakeet
nodes.summary: Some brains don't feel time passing — deadlines arrive as surprises. Parakeet is the friendly but persistent voice that keeps deadlines visible without shame, escalating tone only when urgency truly warrants it.

nodes.label: squirrel:recover
nodes.name: SKILL
nodes.title: squirrel:recover
nodes.summary: Recover cognitive context from a forgotten session. The user closed Claude without running `/sq-end` — this skill reconstructs what happened using the edit manifest or Claude's own session history, then offers to persist it.

nodes.label: squirrel:session-start
nodes.name: SKILL
nodes.title: squirrel:session-start
nodes.summary: Solve the context-switching problem. When the user returns to a project (after hours, days, or weeks), this skill rebuilds the cognitive context FAST by reading the vault's structured notes.

nodes.label: squirrel:hyperfocus-guardian
nodes.name: SKILL
nodes.title: squirrel:hyperfocus-guardian
nodes.summary: Hyperfocus is a double-edged sword: productive streaks can become unsustainable tunnel vision that leads to burnout, missed meals, forgotten commitments, and poor decision-making late in a session. This skill intervenes gently but firmly to impose a circuit breaker.

nodes.label: squirrel:session-end
nodes.name: SKILL
nodes.title: squirrel:session-end
nodes.summary: Prevent context loss between sessions. Every time the user closes a coding session, capture the cognitive state in a structured shutdown note. This is the SINGLE MOST IMPORTANT skill — without it, the system fails.

nodes.label: squirrel:sync-in
nodes.name: SKILL
nodes.title: squirrel:sync-in
nodes.summary: Receive a context package from the OTHER environment (personal ↔ work) and apply it to the LOCAL vault safely. The user pasted the package — your job is to validate, show diff, ask, apply.

nodes.label: squirrel:sync-out
nodes.name: SKILL
nodes.title: squirrel:sync-out
nodes.summary: Bridge two air-gapped environments (personal/corporate) by generating a self-contained, hash-verified Markdown block. The user manually transfers via clipboard or email. The other side applies via `sync-in`.

nodes.label: squirrel:where-am-i (v0.3.0 — fully script-driven)
nodes.name: SKILL
nodes.title: squirrel:where-am-i (v0.3.0 — fully script-driven)
nodes.summary: All logic, data collection, and formatting live in `lib/where_am_i_formatter.py`. The skill is a single shell call.

nodes.label: squirrel:task-initiation
nodes.name: SKILL
nodes.title: squirrel:task-initiation
nodes.summary: Task initiation is one of the most debilitating executive function deficits. The **Wall of Awful** (Mahan 2017) — the accumulated emotional weight around a task — can make starting feel physically impossible even when the user knows exactly what to do.

nodes.label: squirrel:brief
nodes.name: SKILL
nodes.title: squirrel:brief
nodes.summary: Generate a clear, structured status of a project that the user can: - Read themselves to remember where they are - Send to a stakeholder (lead, PM, client) - Use as a session-start summary - Include in a sync-out package

nodes.label: squirrel:capture
nodes.name: SKILL
nodes.title: squirrel:capture
nodes.summary: Persist transient context from the agent session into the local Markdown vault so it survives session boundaries, model context windows, and machine restarts. This is the primary "external memory" entry point for the user.

nodes.label: squirrel:challenge
nodes.name: SKILL
nodes.title: squirrel:challenge
nodes.summary: Before a decision, the most useful voice is often the user's own past self — the version who already tried this and hit a wall. This skill makes the vault **argue back**: it retrieves same-subject notes that carry a pushback signal (a decision the user reversed, a session that stalled, a capture tha

nodes.label: squirrel:daily
nodes.name: SKILL
nodes.title: squirrel:daily
nodes.summary: Produce a **per-directory** semantic summary of everything the user did across their AI coding agents during a time window (default: the last 24 h). One directory with 1 session or 20 sessions yields exactly **one** summary — never one per session. The digest is appended to a **weekly** note in the

nodes.label: squirrel:drift
nodes.name: SKILL
nodes.title: squirrel:drift
nodes.summary: The recurring blocker or theme showing up across *unrelated* projects is exactly what the user can't name themselves. This skill clusters recent notes by shared tags and shared `[[wikilink]]` targets, surfaces concepts that recur across 3+ notes whose **projects differ** (R-5.2), and writes a single

nodes.label: squirrel:ghost
nodes.name: SKILL
nodes.title: squirrel:ghost
nodes.summary: Externalizing "what would I say?" helps the user *start* (it pairs with task-initiation). But a confident wrong answer in their own voice is worse than no answer — so this skill is built around guardrails, not fluency:

nodes.label: squirrel:doctor
nodes.name: SKILL
nodes.title: squirrel:doctor
nodes.summary: After an install or upgrade, almost every failure a user hits maps to one of a small set of known causes: a stale background backend squatting on the port, a Gatekeeper/notarization block, a stale app bundle that won the launch race, a `PATH` gap, or a config left untouched on upgrade. This skill tu

nodes.label: squirrel:emerge
nodes.name: SKILL
nodes.title: squirrel:emerge
nodes.summary: Ideas rot in captures with no promotion path. `sq-think drift` names what *recurs* across unrelated projects; `sq-project emerge` asks the next question — **which recurring cluster is ripe to become a project?** It reuses drift's exact clustering (it does NOT fork it), scores each cluster, and prese

nodes.label: squirrel:focus
nodes.name: SKILL
nodes.title: squirrel:focus
nodes.summary: User-driven pin of a `(project + intent)` pair into the **today** or **this-week** manual-focus slot.

nodes.label: squirrel:decision
nodes.name: SKILL
nodes.title: squirrel:decision
nodes.summary: Decisions get lost in chat logs. Capture them explicitly with their context, alternatives considered, and consequences — so future-self and stakeholders can understand WHY a path was taken.

nodes.label: squirrel:connect
nodes.name: SKILL
nodes.title: squirrel:connect
nodes.summary: Cross-project links are invisible when every project is its own island. Given two conceptual TAGs, this skill surfaces the *path* between them over the vault link graph (`[[wikilinks]]`, treated as undirected) plus the notes they share — connections the user would never spot by scrolling. Read-only

nodes.label: squirrel:ingest
nodes.name: SKILL
nodes.title: squirrel:ingest
nodes.summary: External knowledge (PDFs, screenshots, stray `.md` files) never enters the vault except by manual retyping. This skill turns one file into one normal capture — tagged, provenanced, recency-marked — so it is as findable and trustworthy as a hand-written note. Extraction happens **inside the agent** v

nodes.label: Squirrel Post-it Semantics
nodes.name: SKILL
nodes.title: Squirrel Post-it Semantics
nodes.summary: Treat Post-its as a separate capture artifact from both Quick Tasks and project/task notes.

nodes.label: squirrel:hyperfocus-guardian
nodes.name: SKILL
nodes.title: squirrel:hyperfocus-guardian
nodes.summary: Hyperfocus is a double-edged sword: productive streaks can become unsustainable tunnel vision that leads to burnout, missed meals, forgotten commitments, and poor decision-making late in a session. This skill intervenes gently but firmly to impose a circuit breaker.

nodes.label: squirrel:recover
nodes.name: SKILL
nodes.title: squirrel:recover
nodes.summary: Recover cognitive context from a forgotten session. The user closed Claude without running `/sq-end` — this skill reconstructs what happened using the edit manifest or Claude's own session history, then offers to persist it.

nodes.label: squirrel:chunk-task
nodes.name: SKILL
nodes.title: squirrel:chunk-task
nodes.summary: A single large intent labeled "implement auth" or "refactor database layer" is invisible to the The brain — too abstract to start. This skill converts it into a concrete sequence of chunks, each ≤60 min, with domain-specific names the user recognizes.

nodes.label: squirrel:parakeet
nodes.name: SKILL
nodes.title: squirrel:parakeet
nodes.summary: Some brains don't feel time passing — deadlines arrive as surprises. Parakeet is the friendly but persistent voice that keeps deadlines visible without shame, escalating tone only when urgency truly warrants it.

nodes.label: squirrel:graduate
nodes.name: SKILL
nodes.title: squirrel:graduate
nodes.summary: Graduation is the deliberate act of turning a recurring idea into a tracked project — and it must leave a trail back to where it came from. This skill wraps the existing new-project mechanics (`POST /api/projects`, or `POST /api/post-it/<id>/convert` for a Post-it), then writes `[[wikilinks]]` from

nodes.label: squirrel:research
nodes.name: SKILL
nodes.title: squirrel:research
nodes.summary: Research findings evaporate the moment a chat ends. This skill turns a topic into ONE durable, findable **dossier capture** in the vault — a short summary, verbatim source citations, recency markers, and a `confidence` field — so the thinking survives the session.

nodes.label: squirrel-migrate-vault
nodes.name: SKILL
nodes.title: squirrel-migrate-vault
nodes.summary: Bring an existing Obsidian vault into squirrel without losing anything and without touching the original. The migrator maps free-form notes onto the structures squirrel's readers understand (projects, intents, captures, daily notes) so they show up in `/sq-status`, deadlines, and dashboards.

nodes.label: squirrel:new-project (v0.1.0 — script-driven)
nodes.name: SKILL
nodes.title: squirrel:new-project (v0.1.0 — script-driven)
nodes.summary: All validation and writes live in `lib/new_project_writer.py`. This skill parses the user's request, builds the argument list, runs the script, and prints its stdout verbatim.

nodes.label: squirrel:reconcile
nodes.name: SKILL
nodes.title: squirrel:reconcile
nodes.summary: A vault quietly accumulates decisions and captures that contradict each other — you decided Postgres in June and Redis in July and never wrote down which won. This skill retrieves PAIRS of notes about the *same subject* (the ONE canonical definition, R-3.0), shows each conflict with a verbatim quote

nodes.label: squirrel:session-end
nodes.name: SKILL
nodes.title: squirrel:session-end
nodes.summary: Prevent context loss between sessions. Every time the user closes a coding session, capture the cognitive state in a structured shutdown note. This is the SINGLE MOST IMPORTANT skill — without it, the system fails.

nodes.label: squirrel:sync-in
nodes.name: SKILL
nodes.title: squirrel:sync-in
nodes.summary: Receive a context package from the OTHER environment (personal ↔ work) and apply it to the LOCAL vault safely. The user pasted the package — your job is to validate, show diff, ask, apply.

nodes.label: squirrel:session-start
nodes.name: SKILL
nodes.title: squirrel:session-start
nodes.summary: Solve the context-switching problem. When the user returns to a project (after hours, days, or weeks), this skill rebuilds the cognitive context FAST by reading the vault's structured notes.

nodes.label: squirrel:sync-out
nodes.name: SKILL
nodes.title: squirrel:sync-out
nodes.summary: Bridge two air-gapped environments (personal/corporate) by generating a self-contained, hash-verified Markdown block. The user manually transfers via clipboard or email. The other side applies via `sync-in`.

nodes.label: squirrel:task-initiation
nodes.name: SKILL
nodes.title: squirrel:task-initiation
nodes.summary: Task initiation is one of the most debilitating executive function deficits. The **Wall of Awful** (Mahan 2017) — the accumulated emotional weight around a task — can make starting feel physically impossible even when the user knows exactly what to do.

nodes.label: squirrel:trace
nodes.name: SKILL
nodes.title: squirrel:trace
nodes.summary: The user cannot hold the *arc* of an idea in working memory. This skill externalizes it: given a TAG (or a topic), it gathers the relevant notes from the link graph, orders them by when they first appeared, and asks `claude -p` to render a chronological **timeline** — first appearance → key evolutio

nodes.label: squirrel:where-am-i (v0.3.0 — fully script-driven)
nodes.name: SKILL
nodes.title: squirrel:where-am-i (v0.3.0 — fully script-driven)
nodes.summary: All logic, data collection, and formatting live in `lib/where_am_i_formatter.py`. The skill is a single shell call.

nodes.title: 🐿️ Squirrel — Daily Focus
